title: Early-warning signals for critical transitions
authors: Scheffer, M., Bascompte, J., Brock, W.A., Brovkin, V., Carpenter, S.R., Dakos, V., Held, H., van Nes, E.H., Rietkerk, M., & Sugihara, G.
claim_supported: Establishes variance and lag-1 autocorrelation (AC1) as the canonical generic EWS baselines the paper competes against

title: Anticipating critical transitions
authors: Scheffer, M., Carpenter, S.R., Lenton, T.M., Bascompte, J., Brock, W., Dakos, V., van de Koppel, J., van de Leemput, I.A., Levin, S.A., van Nes, E.H., Pascual, M., & Vandermeer, J.
claim_supported: Justifies the cross-disciplinary CSD theoretical framing within which the criterion sits

title: Early warnings of regime shifts: A whole-ecosystem experiment
authors: Carpenter, S.R., Cole, J.J., Pace, M.L., Batt, R., Brock, W.A., Cline, T., Coloso, J., Hodgson, J.R., Kitchell, J.F., Seekell, D.A., Smith, L., & Weidel, B.
claim_supported: Validates variance and AC1 as empirically verified baselines with experimental proof

title: Methods for detecting early warnings of critical transitions in time series illustrated using simulated ecological data
authors: Dakos, V., Carpenter, S.R., Brock, W.A., Ellison, A.M., Guttal, V., Ives, A.R., Kefi, S., Livina, V., Seekell, D.A., van Nes, E.H., & Scheffer, M.
claim_supported: Defines the methodological benchmark toolkit (earlywarnings R package) the paper matches or beats

title: Early warning signals and the prosecutor's fallacy
authors: Boettiger, C., & Hastings, A.
claim_supported: Identifies the prosecutor's fallacy as a systematic false-positive source in generic EWS; directly motivates matched FP design

title: Catastrophic shifts in ecosystems
authors: Scheffer, M., Carpenter, S., Foley, J.A., Folke, C., & Walker, B.
claim_supported: Provides the foundational vocabulary of catastrophic shift, hysteresis, and loss of viable state

title: Resilience and stability of ecological systems
authors: Holling, C.S.
venue: Annual Review of Ecology and Systematics
claim_supported: Grounds the viable state space language; distinguishes collapse as loss of Holling resilience from temporary displacement

title: Catastrophic cascade of failures in interdependent networks
authors: Buldyrev, S.V., Parshani, R., Paul, G., Stanley, H.E., & Havlin, S.
claim_supported: Supports framing of recursive cascading no-progress as a distinct first-order failure mode

title: Universal resilience patterns in complex networks
authors: Gao, J., Barzel, B., & Barabasi, A.-L.
claim_supported: Directly supports use of a scalar criterion (G, p, delta) to characterize collapse proximity in a high-dimensional system

title: Resilience, adaptability and transformability in social-ecological systems
authors: Walker, B., Holling, C.S., Carpenter, S.R., & Kinzig, A.
claim_supported: Provides the exact vocabulary of viable state space, latitude, and precariousness; licenses stability-landscape geometry

title: Blockbuster culture's next rise or fall: The impact of recommender systems on sales diversity
authors: Fleder, D., & Hosanagar, K.
claim_supported: Establishes the sales diversity narrowing phenomenon that motivates using MovieLens to test recursive collapse

title: How algorithmic confounding in recommendation systems increases homogeneity and decreases utility
authors: Chaney, A.J.B., Stewart, B.M., & Engelhardt, B.E.
claim_supported: Key methodological precedent: establishes MovieLens as a confounded dataset generated under collaborative filtering

title: Degenerate feedback loops in recommender systems
authors: Jiang, R., Chiappa, S., Lattimore, T., Gyorgy, A., & Kohli, P.
claim_supported: Theoretical anchor: recursive collapse in recommenders is structural degeneracy, not a bug; degenerate fixed-point attractor

title: Feedback loop and bias amplification in recommender systems
authors: Mansoury, M., Abdollahpouri, H., Pechenizkiy, M., Mobasher, B., & Burke, R.
claim_supported: Provides the direct empirical baseline for the MovieLens experiment: quantifies round-by-round bias amplification

title: Market liquidity and funding liquidity
authors: Brunnermeier, M.K., & Pedersen, L.H.
claim_supported: Theoretical backbone for Volmageddon and March 2020 events as liquidity-spiral phenomena; warning criterion measures pre-spiral fragility

title: Volmageddon and the failure of short volatility products
authors: Augustin, P., Chen, C., & Van den Bergen, H.
claim_supported: Canonical peer-reviewed post-mortem of Volmageddon; establishes the event timeline and recursive feedback loop anatomy
notes: SSRN: 3819342 also listed | manually verified metadata

title: Understanding the VIX
authors: Whaley, R.E.
claim_supported: Provides methodological basis for interpreting VIX level and spikes as market-stress indicators
notes: listed as 2000/2009; DOI given for 2009 update; original 2000 paper titled The investor fear gauge (JPM 26:3); verify which to cite primarily | manually verified metadata

title: Report of the Market-Wide Circuit Breaker (MWCB) Working Group
authors: NYSE Market-Wide Circuit Breaker Working Group
claim_supported: Provides officially documented ground-truth event labels for March 2020 circuit-breaker collapse test cases
notes: institutional report; not peer-reviewed | Clear false DOI/citation match from API; use official report/source manually.

title: Findings Regarding the Market Events of May 6, 2010
authors: CFTC and SEC Staff
claim_supported: Establishes 2010 Flash Crash as a recognized officially documented feedback-driven microstructure collapse
notes: institutional report; not peer-reviewed | Clear false DOI/citation match from API; use official report/source manually.

title: Can ETFs Contribute to Systemic Risk?
authors: ESRB Advisory Scientific Committee
claim_supported: Provides regulatory backing for VIX ETPs as systemic risk vectors; strengthens Volmageddon as a genuine systemic event
notes: institutional report; ESRB Advisory Scientific Committee Reports No. 9; not peer-reviewed

title: The Lean 4 Theorem Prover and Programming Language
authors: de Moura, L., & Ullrich, S.
claim_supported: Establishes Lean 4 as the specific version of the proof assistant used; Lean 4 kernel is the trust anchor
notes: cite over Lean 3 paper if artifact is in Lean 4

title: The Lean mathematical library
authors: The mathlib Community
claim_supported: Required citation whenever import Mathlib appears in Lean code; justifies reliance on mathlib infrastructure

title: Formally verified mathematics
authors: Avigad, J., & Harrison, J.
claim_supported: Justifies why a Lean-verified obstruction is a meaningful artifact beyond informal proof; kernel-checked proofs are epistemically superior

title: Mathematics and the formal turn
authors: Avigad, J.
venue: Bulletin of the American Mathematical Society
claim_supported: Up-to-date survey placing the Lean-verified result within the contemporary landscape of formalized mathematics
notes: DOI not provided in source; available via AMS; arXiv 2311.00007

title: A formal proof of the Kepler conjecture
authors: Hales, T., Adams, M., Bauer, G., Dang, T.D., Harrison, J., Hoang, L.T., et al.
claim_supported: Canonical precedent for a formally verified proof accepted as completed mathematical proof in a research journal
notes: verified using HOL Light / Isabelle; analogy to Lean applies

title: Quantifying limits to detection of early warning for critical transitions
authors: Boettiger, C., & Hastings, A.
venue: Journal of the Royal Society Interface
claim_supported: Introduces power analysis and ROC trade-offs for comparing EWS indicators; grounds the locked false-positive contract methodologically
notes: Cluster 1: ROC/AUC/Power Analysis; different paper from EWS #7 (that paper DOI 10.1098/rspb.2012.2085) | manually verified metadata

title: The meaning and use of the area under a receiver operating characteristic (ROC) curve
authors: Hanley, J.A., & McNeil, B.J.
claim_supported: Foundational ROC curve paper; required citation when AUC comparisons are used to evaluate competing predictors at matched false-positive rates

title: AI models collapse when trained on recursively generated data
authors: Shumailov, I., Shumaylov, Z., Zhao, Y., Papernot, N., Anderson, R., & Gal, Y.
claim_supported: Directly proves recursive collapse (variance collapse, diversity loss) in generative systems trained on their own outputs; closest existing paper to this contribution
notes: Cluster 3: Model Collapse in Recursive Generative Systems; must-cite per Perplexity

title: Systematically false positives in early warning signal analysis
authors: Diks, C., Hommes, C., & Wang, J.
claim_supported: Identifies entire classes of systems that always produce rising variance/AC1 without approaching transition; essential for justifying matched FP design
notes: Cluster 6: Systematic False Positives in EWS
